message:
feat(supervision): add configurable tolerance to RestartStormMonitor

StormMonitorConfig gains a tolerance field (default 1.2) that controls
the normalizer in the likelihood ratio computation. Intensities below
tolerance * expected_rate cause the evidence to decay rather than
accumulate, reducing false alarms from mild variance above the expected
restart rate.

Previously the normalizer was implicit/hardcoded. Making it explicit
allows operators to tune sensitivity: a higher tolerance (e.g. 1.5)
tolerates more variance but reacts slower to genuine storms, while a
lower tolerance (e.g. 1.05) is more sensitive but risks false alerts.

Includes test verifying custom tolerance behavior in the monitor's
evidence accumulation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
